title: Born And Raised

jokes.text: Sweetums Cares, a non-profit group, that puts umbrella hats on homeless people when it rains
jokes.setup: Expecting a typical corporate sponsor message about charitable work
jokes.punchline: The bizarre specificity of umbrella hats for homeless people
jokes.explanation: Excellent absurdist humor with perfect specificity. The umbrella hat detail is unexpected and memorable.

dialogue.speaker: Radio Announcer
dialogue.line: This message brought to you by the Pawnee Chamber of Commerce. We're committed to helping those in need. That's why we've donated over 10,000 umbrella hats to homeless people in our community.

dialogue.speaker: Radio Announcer
dialogue.line: Because nothing says 'we care' like a hat that's also an umbrella. Specifically designed so homeless people can stay dry while remaining completely unable to use their hands.

jokes.text: David Parker, who took off for eight months to study the migration patterns of our nation's squirrels. We have not seen him since.
jokes.setup: Normal radio host vacation announcement
jokes.punchline: The absurd squirrel migration study and the ominous 'not seen him since'
jokes.explanation: Good absurdist premise with deadpan delivery. The escalation from silly study to missing person is well-crafted.

dialogue.speaker: Derry Murbles
dialogue.line: We'll be back after these messages. I'm taking a vacation to study the squirrel migration patterns in the greater Pawnee area.

dialogue.speaker: Derry Murbles
dialogue.line: My research assistant Gary was supposed to join me, but I haven't seen him since we entered the woods three days ago.

jokes.text: we removed a lot of my poems and emotional ramblings and pictures of unicorns
jokes.setup: Discussion of editing a professional historical guide
jokes.punchline: The inappropriate content that had to be removed reveals Leslie's personality
jokes.explanation: Perfect character moment that efficiently establishes Leslie's earnest but unprofessional tendencies.

dialogue.speaker: Leslie
dialogue.line: I was editing the Pawnee Historical Society's guide, and I found some really inappropriate content that had to be removed.

dialogue.speaker: Leslie
dialogue.line: There was this whole section I wrote about how Tom Haverford's great-great-grandfather was a suspected horse thief, and I may have added some editorial commentary about how 'the apple doesn't fall far from the tree.'

jokes.text: could one say that a book is nothing more than a painting of words, which are the notes on the tapestry of the greatest film ever sculpted?
jokes.setup: Pretentious radio host attempting deep artistic commentary
jokes.punchline: The completely nonsensical metaphor mixing incompatible art forms
jokes.explanation: Excellent parody of pretentious art speak. The mixing of metaphors is deliberately confusing and funny.

dialogue.speaker: Derry Murbles
dialogue.line: You see, the brushstrokes of Monet are like the percussion section of a Debussy composition—both are essentially sandwiches.

jokes.text: One could say that. But should one?
jokes.setup: Derry's nonsensical artistic question
jokes.punchline: Leslie's polite but pointed dismissal
jokes.explanation: Perfect economy and structure. Leslie's diplomatic but cutting response is character-appropriate.

dialogue.speaker: Leslie Knope
dialogue.line: That's a great question, and I appreciate you asking it, but I'm going to stop you right there because I have no idea what you just said.

jokes.text: the lesbian Afro-Norwegian funk duo, Nefertiti's Fjord
jokes.setup: Standard radio show music outro
jokes.punchline: The impossibly specific and ridiculous band description
jokes.explanation: Wonderfully absurd band name that perfectly captures the show's commitment to specific details.

jokes.text: Oh, wow. They are terrible. Yes, they're quite awful, but they are lesbians. So...
jokes.setup: The awful music playing in background
jokes.punchline: The awkward justification for supporting terrible music
jokes.explanation: Good cringe moment that captures the awkward liberal guilt of the characters.

dialogue.speaker: Leslie
dialogue.line: I know this music is terrible, but we have to support local artists.

dialogue.speaker: Derry Murbles
dialogue.line: Leslie, that's just a guy playing a kazoo in the corner.

dialogue.speaker: Leslie
dialogue.line: A LOCAL guy playing a kazoo in the corner.

jokes.text: Usually, I only read nautical novels and my own personal manifestos
jokes.setup: Ron making an exception to read Leslie's book
jokes.punchline: The revelation of his bizarre reading habits
jokes.explanation: Perfect Ron Swanson line that reveals character while being genuinely funny. The manifestos detail is perfect.

dialogue.speaker: Ron
dialogue.line: I have made an exception and read your book.

dialogue.speaker: Leslie
dialogue.line: Really? What did you think?

dialogue.speaker: Ron
dialogue.line: It was well-written. Engaging. I read it in one sitting.

dialogue.speaker: Leslie
dialogue.line: That's wonderful!

dialogue.speaker: Ron
dialogue.line: I was in the bathtub. Naked. For seven hours.

jokes.text: I started thinking about you as a woman and as a person, and I got carried away.
jokes.setup: Leslie explaining why her inscription is seven pages long
jokes.punchline: The awkward phrasing that makes it sound romantic
jokes.explanation: Good character moment that shows Leslie's tendency to overshare and her awkward phrasing.

dialogue.speaker: Leslie
dialogue.line: Well, I wanted to make sure it was comprehensive. I included all of my accomplishments, my philosophy on public service, and a detailed timeline of the Pawnee Parks Department's greatest achievements.

dialogue.speaker: Ben
dialogue.line: Leslie, it's a plaque.

dialogue.speaker: Leslie
dialogue.line: I know that, Ben. But I also wanted to make it personal. So I added some reflections on what this job has meant to me, how it's shaped who I am, and how grateful I am for everyone who's supported me along the way.

dialogue.speaker: Ben
dialogue.line: Okay, but the part that concerns me is where you wrote, 'I will never stop thinking about this job. It consumes my every waking moment. I dream about it at night.'

dialogue.speaker: Leslie
dialogue.line: What's wrong with that?

dialogue.speaker: Ben
dialogue.line: It sounds like you're in love with the Parks Department.

dialogue.speaker: Leslie
dialogue.line: I AM in love with the Parks Department!

jokes.text: Okay, mine just says, 'Get well soon.' Aren't you sick? No. Something's off.
jokes.setup: Contrasting Leslie's long inscription with a simple one
jokes.punchline: Ron's completely inappropriate and confusing message
jokes.explanation: Excellent Ron moment that's both unexpected and perfectly in character. The deadpan confusion is perfect.

dialogue.speaker: Ron
dialogue.line: You son of a bitch.

jokes.text: I can read over 3,000 words a minute, with total comprehension. One time, I read all of Siddhartha at a traffic stop.
jokes.setup: Character claiming to be a speed reader
jokes.punchline: The impossibly fast reading speed and absurd traffic stop story
jokes.explanation: Good absurdist claim with specific details that make it funnier. The Siddhartha reference adds literary pretension.

dialogue.speaker: April
dialogue.line: I'm a speed reader. I can read 20,000 words per minute.

dialogue.speaker: Leslie
dialogue.line: April, that's not possible.

dialogue.speaker: April
dialogue.line: I read the entire traffic code once. Got pulled over for speeding. Cop said I was going 150 in a 35. I told him I was just speed reading the road signs.

dialogue.speaker: Ron
dialogue.line: Did it work?

dialogue.speaker: April
dialogue.line: No. He gave me a ticket for speeding and one for reckless endangerment.

jokes.text: I brought some Entertainment 720 pillowcases for your pillow. Never Stop Dreaming. Tom Haverford. I never do. Chris Traeger. Respect. Game recognize game.
jokes.setup: Tom bringing promotional materials
jokes.punchline: The ridiculous pillowcase gift and Chris's earnest response
jokes.explanation: Good character interaction showing Tom's entrepreneurial absurdity and Chris's positivity.

dialogue.speaker: Tom
dialogue.line: I made promotional pillowcases for the Parks Department. Check it out.

dialogue.speaker: Chris
dialogue.line: Tom, this pillowcase has a picture of a sewage treatment plant on it.

dialogue.speaker: Tom
dialogue.line: Yeah, I thought it really captures the essence of what we do here.

dialogue.speaker: Chris
dialogue.line: I appreciate the effort, Tom, I really do. But I'm not sure people want to rest their heads on a picture of raw sewage.

jokes.text: The Time-Traveler's Optometrist, by Pawnee's own Penelope Foster. A heartwarming story about a caveman eye doctor who travels to present-day Cincinnati and can see everything but love.
jokes.setup: Description of a local book club selection
jokes.punchline: The impossibly ridiculous premise and cheesy romantic twist
jokes.explanation: Brilliant parody of terrible romance novels with specific absurd details. The 'see everything but love' line is perfect.

jokes.text: Unreadable. Then, Joan slaps her sticker on it. Best-seller four years in a row.
jokes.setup: The terrible book description
jokes.punchline: The power of Joan's endorsement despite quality
jokes.explanation: Good commentary on how media influence works regardless of quality. Efficient setup and payoff.

dialogue.speaker: Tom
dialogue.line: "From the moment I picked your book up until I laid it down, I was convulsed with laughter. Some day I hope to read it." - Groucho Marx

dialogue.speaker: Tom
dialogue.line: But you know what? Joan from accounting put this five-star review on Amazon, and now it's a bestseller.

jokes.text: Well, she's not going to 'getch' me.
jokes.setup: Tom warning about Joan's 'gotcha' moments
jokes.punchline: Leslie's defiant but awkward phrasing
jokes.explanation: Mild wordplay that works because it's awkward and shows Leslie's determination.

dialogue.speaker: Tom
dialogue.line: Joan Callamezzo has these 'gotcha' moments where she tries to trip you up.

dialogue.speaker: Leslie
dialogue.line: Well, I'm not going to fall for it. I'm prepared for anything she throws at me. Bring it on, Joan. I welcome the challenge. I'm not afraid.

jokes.text: If there's one thing Leslie's not, it's sloppy. She's also not brief. This is going to take forever.
jokes.setup: Discussing whether Leslie made mistakes
jokes.punchline: The backhanded compliment about her thoroughness
jokes.explanation: Good character observation that's both complimentary and complaining. Efficient delivery.

dialogue.speaker: Ben
dialogue.line: Look, Leslie makes mistakes like everyone else, but her thoroughness is actually her greatest strength.

dialogue.speaker: Jerry
dialogue.line: So... you're saying she's thorough?

dialogue.speaker: Ben
dialogue.line: Yes.

dialogue.speaker: Jerry
dialogue.line: That's not a compliment, Ben. That's what we say about people we don't like.

jokes.text: Here we go. Helping Leslie. Okey-doke.
jokes.setup: More people arriving to help fact-check
jokes.punchline: April and Andy's complete lack of enthusiasm
jokes.explanation: Simple but effective character moment showing their reluctant participation.

dialogue.speaker: April
dialogue.line: Why are all these people here?

dialogue.speaker: Andy
dialogue.line: I don't know. I guess they heard we needed help.

dialogue.speaker: April
dialogue.line: Well, tell them to leave.

dialogue.speaker: Andy
dialogue.line: I don't want to hurt their feelings.

dialogue.speaker: April
dialogue.line: I do.

jokes.text: Hi. Joan Callamezzo. Yeah. Hi, Ben Wyatt. We've met. I don't think so. Yeah, I was on your show. Nope.
jokes.setup: Ben trying to remind Joan they've met
jokes.punchline: Joan's complete dismissal despite clear evidence
jokes.explanation: Good cringe moment that establishes Joan's self-absorbed personality through her dismissiveness.

dialogue.speaker: Ben
dialogue.line: Joan, we've actually met before. Remember? At the city council meeting last month?

dialogue.speaker: Joan
dialogue.line: I have no idea who you are.

dialogue.speaker: Ben
dialogue.line: But you were sitting right next to me. We talked for twenty minutes about zoning regulations.

dialogue.speaker: Joan
dialogue.line: Doesn't ring a bell.

jokes.text: I'm trying to find the words to describe this girl Without being disrespectful
jokes.setup: Joan appearing to be thoughtful about Leslie
jokes.punchline: The revealing pause and half-sung delivery suggests she's about to be disrespectful anyway
jokes.explanation: Sets up Joan's fake politeness. The musical delivery suggests insincerity.

jokes.text: At the risk of bragging, one of the things I'm best at is riding coattails. Behind every successful man, is me, smiling and taking partial credit.
jokes.setup: Tom talking about his business skills
jokes.punchline: His honest admission about being a parasite presented as a skill
jokes.explanation: Excellent character moment where Tom's self-awareness makes his parasitic behavior funnier.

dialogue.speaker: Tom
dialogue.line: I'm a parasite. I feed off the success of others and I'm very good at it.

jokes.text: Ben, I'm cute together with everybody.
jokes.setup: Someone commenting they look cute together
jokes.punchline: Joan's narcissistic response
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Joan's vanity and self-absorption efficiently.

dialogue.speaker: Joan
dialogue.line: Of course we do. I'm adorable and he has the good sense to stand next to me.

jokes.text: Yeah, actually, in here it says that Pawnee is great. But in reality, it's terrible.
jokes.setup: Looking for factual errors in Leslie's book
jokes.punchline: April's nihilistic take on the fundamental premise
jokes.explanation: Perfect April line that's both funny and character-appropriate. The deadpan delivery makes it work.

dialogue.speaker: April
dialogue.line: Actually, there's a huge factual error in your book. You say that the government exists to serve the people. That's not true. The government exists to take our money and tell us what to do.

jokes.text: I've never used a phone in my life.
jokes.setup: Andy asking about phones
jokes.punchline: Ron's extreme anti-technology stance
jokes.explanation: Perfect Ron character moment. The extremeness is absurd but totally believable for him.

dialogue.speaker: Ron
dialogue.line: I have no use for a phone. I don't want people calling me, and I certainly don't want to call them.

dialogue.speaker: Ron
dialogue.line: If something is important, they can find me in person. If it's not important, I don't care about it.

jokes.text: Here's my goal. I'm going to get these two people that I've known for a long time to talk to me for five minutes. Who am I kidding? One minute. One minute would be amazing.
jokes.setup: Andy talking to camera about his relationships
jokes.punchline: The pathetically low bar he sets for social interaction
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Andy's awareness of his social standing with April and Ron.

dialogue.speaker: Andy
dialogue.line: I have really high standards when it comes to dating. A woman has to be intelligent, beautiful, independent... Actually, no. A woman just has to talk to me. That's it. That's the bar. If a woman has ever spoken words in my direction, I'm convinced we're soulmates.

jokes.text: Oh... Oh...Whoa! Where should... Over here? Back here? Where is it now? Come on. Stick it. Ooh! Hang on Stick the sticker. No, no, no. Not so fast. Put it on.
jokes.setup: Joan teasing Leslie with the book club sticker
jokes.punchline: Leslie's desperate physical comedy trying to get the sticker
jokes.explanation: Physical comedy that shows Leslie's desperation. More effective visually than in transcript form.

dialogue.speaker: Joan
dialogue.line: You want this book club sticker?

dialogue.speaker: Leslie
dialogue.line: Yes! Please, I need it for my binder!

dialogue.speaker: Joan
dialogue.line: Too bad.

dialogue.speaker: Leslie
dialogue.line: *lunges desperately* Come on, Joan! *falls over* Please! *crawls* I'm begging you!

jokes.text: We received a tip that you, Leslie 'Born and Raised' Knope, were not born in Pawnee. What? Gotcha!
jokes.setup: Joan building up to her reveal
jokes.punchline: The dramatic gotcha moment
jokes.explanation: Good setup and payoff for the episode's central conflict. Joan's theatrical delivery works.

jokes.text: I have to tell you. This feels like 'Gotcha' journalism. In what way? That way. You put 'Gotcha' on my face.
jokes.setup: Leslie complaining about gotcha journalism
jokes.punchline: The literal 'Gotcha' graphic on her face
jokes.explanation: Good visual gag that works on multiple levels - literal and metaphorical gotcha journalism.

dialogue.speaker: Leslie
dialogue.line: I can't believe they used gotcha journalism on me. That's so unfair and unethical.

dialogue.speaker: Joan
dialogue.line: Well, look on the bright side.

dialogue.speaker: Leslie
dialogue.line: What bright side? My reputation is ruined!

dialogue.speaker: Joan
dialogue.line: At least you got a cool graphic out of it.

jokes.text: Where was Leslie Knope actually born? Pawnee. We will pull out the world map and speculate wildly.
jokes.setup: Teasing upcoming investigation
jokes.punchline: The absurd methodology and confident wrong answer
jokes.explanation: Good parody of sensationalist journalism with the admission they'll just speculate wildly.

jokes.text: Oh, God. Not the 'Gotcha' dancers.
jokes.setup: The over-the-top TV show production
jokes.punchline: Leslie's horrified reaction to the dancers
jokes.explanation: Good visual comedy enhanced by Leslie's mortified reaction. The absurdity of gotcha dancers is perfect.

dialogue.speaker: Leslie
dialogue.line: What are those dancers doing? That's not what we choreographed at all! This is a disaster!

jokes.text: That was despicable I am horrified at her tactics. That said, the show was very lively.
jokes.setup: Chris condemning Joan's journalism
jokes.punchline: His immediate contradiction praising the entertainment value
jokes.explanation: Perfect Chris moment showing his inability to be completely negative about anything.

dialogue.speaker: Chris
dialogue.line: Joan, your journalism is irresponsible and dangerous. You're a menace to this community.

dialogue.speaker: Chris
dialogue.line: That said, that exposé on the mayor was absolutely riveting. I couldn't put it down.

jokes.text: Joan 'Gotcha' Don't it hurt ya
jokes.setup: The gotcha theme
jokes.punchline: Chris creating a catchy jingle
jokes.explanation: Simple but effective character moment showing Chris's positive spin on everything.

dialogue.speaker: Chris
dialogue.line: I've been working on something. I call it 'Treat Yo Self: The Song.' It's catchy, it's got a good beat, and most importantly, it's a gotcha.

dialogue.speaker: Chris
dialogue.line: ♪ Treat yo self, treat yo self, to the finer things in life. Treat yo self, treat yo self, like you're somebody's wife. ♪

dialogue.speaker: Chris
dialogue.line: You see what I did there? It's self-aware, it's humorous, and it's going to be stuck in everyone's heads for days.

jokes.text: When Leslie's in trouble, there's only one man for the job. Bert Macklin, FBI. You thought I was dead? So did the President's enemies.
jokes.setup: Andy offering to help Leslie
jokes.punchline: His elaborate fictional FBI persona
jokes.explanation: Excellent callback to Andy's fictional character. The 'President's enemies' line is perfectly over-the-top.

dialogue.speaker: Andy
dialogue.line: I'm gonna help you out, Leslie. I'm gonna go deep undercover. My name is Agent Bert Macklin, FBI. I have no weaknesses. I can't be killed. And I will find your guy.

jokes.text: You know, I think I'm just gonna hang out here with you guys, if that's cool. It's not. Great. I could leave. I could. But I'm not going to. I will get my one minute of small talk, damn it.
jokes.setup: Andy trying to socialize with April and Ron
jokes.punchline: His determination despite clear rejection
jokes.explanation: Good callback to Andy's earlier goal. His persistence despite rejection is endearing and funny.

dialogue.speaker: Andy
dialogue.line: Hey guys, want to grab lunch? There's this great burger place downtown!

dialogue.speaker: April
dialogue.line: No.

dialogue.speaker: Ron
dialogue.line: Absolutely not.

dialogue.speaker: Andy
dialogue.line: What about dinner then? Or breakfast? I make a mean scrambled egg.

dialogue.speaker: April
dialogue.line: Stop talking.

dialogue.speaker: Ron
dialogue.line: I would rather eat a bowl of nails.

dialogue.speaker: Andy
dialogue.line: Cool, cool, cool. So Thursday?

jokes.text: We wanted her to sign the 'Gotcha' wall.
jokes.setup: Staff trying to get Leslie back
jokes.punchline: The existence of a gotcha wall for victims to sign
jokes.explanation: Good absurdist detail that shows how institutionalized the gotcha journalism has become.

dialogue.speaker: Tom
dialogue.line: We need Leslie back on Pawnee Today. She was the heart of this show.

dialogue.speaker: Jerry
dialogue.line: I have an idea. What if we create a wall where all the people I've gotcha'd can come sign it?

dialogue.speaker: Ben
dialogue.line: Jerry, that's... that's just a wall for your victims.

dialogue.speaker: Jerry
dialogue.line: Exactly! It'll be iconic!

jokes.text: I was the first one to tell you that?
jokes.setup: Tom crediting Ben with basic business advice
jokes.punchline: Ben's incredulous reaction to being credited with the obvious
jokes.explanation: Good reaction beat showing Ben's disbelief at Tom's business naivety.

dialogue.speaker: Tom
dialogue.line: Ben gave me some great business advice.

dialogue.speaker: Ben
dialogue.line: I just said 'don't spend more money than you make.'

dialogue.speaker: Tom
dialogue.line: Exactly.

dialogue.speaker: Ben
dialogue.line: That's not advice, Tom. That's... that's just math.

jokes.text: Joan's a married woman. She wants what she can't have. It's a game of cat and mouse.
jokes.setup: Tom explaining his strategy
jokes.punchline: His confident misreading of the situation (she's divorcing)
jokes.explanation: Good dramatic irony since we know Joan is divorcing. Tom's confidence makes it funnier.

dialogue.speaker: Tom
dialogue.line: She's been distant lately, but I've got a plan. I'm going to surprise her with a romantic dinner, really remind her why she fell for me in the first place.

dialogue.speaker: Tom
dialogue.line: Mark my words, by the end of the night, she'll be signing papers.

dialogue.speaker: Tom
dialogue.line: Divorce papers.

jokes.text: I think I speak on behalf of everyone in the entire world when I say, we need to know the truth about where you were born
jokes.setup: Public book reading about Leslie's birthplace
jokes.punchline: The absurd global importance placed on local politician's birthplace
jokes.explanation: Good escalation showing how ridiculous the controversy has become.

dialogue.speaker: Audience Member
dialogue.line: So where was Leslie Knope born?

dialogue.speaker: Leslie Knope
dialogue.line: Pawnee, Indiana.

dialogue.speaker: Audience Member
dialogue.line: My God. Pawnee, Indiana produced Leslie Knope? That changes everything. That changes *everything*.

jokes.text: You should go back where you came from. I am back from where I came from. That sentence was confusing! You might as well be from China!
jokes.setup: Hostile questioning about Leslie's birthplace
jokes.punchline: The linguistic confusion leading to random xenophobia
jokes.explanation: Good satire of birther conspiracies and how quickly they escalate to xenophobia.

dialogue.speaker: Hostile audience member
dialogue.line: Where are you from?

dialogue.speaker: Leslie
dialogue.line: I'm from Eagleton.

dialogue.speaker: Hostile audience member
dialogue.line: No, where are you REALLY from?

dialogue.speaker: Leslie
dialogue.line: Eagleton, Indiana. It's right next to Pawnee.

dialogue.speaker: Hostile audience member
dialogue.line: That's not a real place.

dialogue.speaker: Leslie
dialogue.line: It's definitely a real place. I grew up there.

dialogue.speaker: Hostile audience member
dialogue.line: Well, you sound foreign. Are you a citizen?

jokes.text: When I was 18, Val Kilmer saw me at a mall and told me I should model. That never happened.
jokes.setup: Joan telling a story about her past
jokes.punchline: Her immediate contradiction of her own lie
jokes.explanation: Excellent character moment showing Joan's pathological lying and lack of self-awareness. Perfect timing.

dialogue.line: Unable to parse - insufficient context provided. The punchline text describing 'her immediate contradiction of her own lie' is not included. Please provide the actual dialogue text to parse.

jokes.text: I'm like a caged peacock yearning for the wind on her haunches.
jokes.setup: Joan describing her sexual appetite
jokes.punchline: The ridiculous peacock metaphor
jokes.explanation: Wonderfully ridiculous metaphor that perfectly captures Joan's pretentiousness and sexual aggression.

dialogue.speaker: Joan
dialogue.line: I have the sexual appetite of a peacock. A peacock that just discovered cocaine.

jokes.text: I'll have another drink, and so will this adorable hunk of caramel to my right.
jokes.setup: Joan getting drunk and aggressive
jokes.punchline: The uncomfortable racial/food metaphor for Ben
jokes.explanation: Good cringe moment showing Joan's inappropriate behavior and objectification.

dialogue.speaker: Joan
dialogue.line: You know what? I'm gonna say it. Ben, you're like a... you're like a wonderbread sandwich.

dialogue.speaker: Ben
dialogue.line: I... what?

dialogue.speaker: Joan
dialogue.line: Yeah. You're white, you're bland, and you get moldy if you don't use you right away.

jokes.text: Is she going to powder her vagina?
jokes.setup: Joan's euphemistic bathroom announcement
jokes.punchline: Tom's literal interpretation
jokes.explanation: Crude but character-appropriate moment showing Tom's panic and literal thinking.

dialogue.speaker: Joan
dialogue.line: I'm going to powder my nose.

dialogue.speaker: Tom
dialogue.line: Your nose doesn't need powder. It looks fine. Are you having a skin condition? Because I know a dermatologist. Well, I know a guy who knows a dermatologist.

jokes.text: You know, nerd culture is mainstream now. So, when you use the word 'nerd' derogatorily, it means you're the one that's out of the Zeitgeist.
jokes.setup: Ben trying to be boring to help Tom
jokes.punchline: His nerdy defense of nerd culture
jokes.explanation: Perfect Ben moment that's both helpful and reveals his character. The irony is he's being nerdy while defending nerds.

dialogue.speaker: Ben
dialogue.line: Look, I know nerds. I am a nerd. And nerds are cool now. We run the world. We make the music, the movies, the TV shows. We invented the internet. We're not the weird kids anymore.

dialogue.speaker: Ben
dialogue.line: And honestly, Tom, trying to be boring is actually way more embarrassing than just being yourself.

jokes.text: Hey, April. I was looking to get some new music. And I was wondering if you could recommend anything. The Internet.
jokes.setup: Andy trying to make conversation with April
jokes.punchline: April's dismissive non-answer
jokes.explanation: Perfect April response - technically helpful but completely dismissive. Good character interaction.

dialogue.speaker: Andy
dialogue.line: So April, what do you like to do for fun?

dialogue.speaker: April
dialogue.line: I like things.

jokes.text: I really like your haircut. Where did you get it? Prison.
jokes.setup: Andy complimenting April's hair
jokes.punchline: April's absurd and dismissive answer
jokes.explanation: Excellent April line that's both funny and completely shuts down conversation. Perfect deadpan delivery.

dialogue.speaker: Andy
dialogue.line: Hey, your hair looks really nice today.

dialogue.speaker: April
dialogue.line: Thanks. I'm growing it out so I have something to hang myself with.

jokes.text: How is your sister doing? She has the shingles. Who is your favorite character on Sex and the City? ALF.
jokes.setup: Andy's continued attempts at conversation
jokes.punchline: April's medical overshare and nonsensical TV reference
jokes.explanation: Great two-part joke. The shingles answer is darkly inappropriate, ALF is completely wrong genre but perfectly April.

dialogue.speaker: Andy
dialogue.line: So what have you been up to?

dialogue.speaker: April
dialogue.line: Well, I had a cyst removed from my ovary last week, and the doctor said it was the size of a grapefruit. Then I watched six hours of Designing Women in a row.

dialogue.speaker: Andy
dialogue.line: That's... I didn't need to know that.

dialogue.speaker: April
dialogue.line: You asked.

jokes.text: You're stranded on a desert island, what is the one thing that you bring with you? Silence.
jokes.setup: Andy's desert island question to Ron
jokes.punchline: Ron's perfectly in-character non-material answer
jokes.explanation: Perfect Ron answer that's both funny and deeply character-appropriate. The concept of bringing silence is brilliant.

dialogue.speaker: Andy
dialogue.line: If you were stranded on a desert island, what's one thing you'd bring?

dialogue.speaker: Ron
dialogue.line: A boat.

jokes.text: 13% think I'm crazy-eyed? What? Why?
jokes.setup: Discussing poll numbers about the birthplace controversy
jokes.punchline: Leslie's outrage at the crazy-eyed perception
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Leslie's priorities and self-image concerns.

dialogue.speaker: Leslie
dialogue.line: What do you mean crazy-eyed? I'm not crazy-eyed!

dialogue.speaker: Ben
dialogue.line: Leslie, in the poll, 34% of respondents described you as 'crazy-eyed.'

dialogue.speaker: Leslie
dialogue.line: Crazy-eyed?! I have very sane eyes! These are the eyes of a rational woman!

jokes.text: Eagleton is the land of rich, snobby jerks. There's a whole chapter about it in my book. I could write a second book about Eagleton and how stupid it is. And I'd call it, Eagleton: The Land of Rich, Snobby Jerks
jokes.setup: Leslie complaining about having to go to Eagleton
jokes.punchline: Her escalating rant and terrible sequel title
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Leslie's petty rivalry. The literal book title is perfectly Leslie.

dialogue.speaker: Leslie
dialogue.line: I have to go to Eagleton. Eagleton! Do you know what Eagleton is? It's the worst place in the world. It's a suburban hellhole full of snobs and idiots and people who don't appreciate parks or nature or anything good. And I have to go there. Me! Leslie Knope! I have to set foot in Eagleton!

dialogue.speaker: Leslie
dialogue.line: This is worse than that time I had to work with Tammy Two. At least Tammy Two is just one person. Eagleton is like a thousand Tammy Twos in khakis.

dialogue.speaker: Leslie
dialogue.line: And if I have to make a movie about this experience, it's going to be called 'Eagleton 2: The Wrath of Khan.' Wait, no. 'Die Hard: With a Vengeance in Eagleton.' Actually, 'Eagleton Forever: The Reckoning.' No! 'Eagleton: The Squeakquel.'

jokes.text: Take it down a notch, Alexis. I need a copy of my birth certificate.
jokes.setup: Alexis giving an overly cheerful greeting
jokes.punchline: Leslie's immediately hostile response
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Leslie's Eagleton prejudice affecting her behavior.

dialogue.speaker: Alexis
dialogue.line: Hey Leslie! How are you doing today?

dialogue.speaker: Leslie
dialogue.line: What do you want?

jokes.text: Wow. That usually works.
jokes.setup: Chris's charm failing to get special treatment
jokes.punchline: His genuine surprise at being refused
jokes.explanation: Perfect Chris moment showing his surprise when his usual charm doesn't work.

dialogue.speaker: Chris
dialogue.line: I'd like to get a table for two without a reservation.

dialogue.speaker: Host
dialogue.line: I'm sorry, sir, we're completely booked.

dialogue.speaker: Chris
dialogue.line: Oh, I think you'll find we can make this work. I'm Chris Traeger, Deputy Director of the Parks Department.

dialogue.speaker: Host
dialogue.line: And I think you'll find we can't.

dialogue.speaker: Chris
dialogue.line: But... I'm extremely likable.

jokes.text: That's broken.
jokes.setup: Andy presumably trying to break into records office
jokes.punchline: His matter-of-fact assessment of breaking something
jokes.explanation: Simple but effective physical comedy. Andy's casual delivery of obvious failure is funny.

dialogue.speaker: Andy
dialogue.line: I'm gonna break into the records office.

dialogue.speaker: Andy
dialogue.line: I'm gonna break something.

dialogue.speaker: Andy
dialogue.line: Probably me.

jokes.text: Take me home, dumpling. It's beddy time.
jokes.setup: Joan completely drunk and inappropriate
jokes.punchline: The infantilizing language and pet names
jokes.explanation: Good cringe moment showing how inappropriately drunk Joan has become.

dialogue.speaker: Joan
dialogue.line: The infantilizing language and pet names

jokes.text: They're making a sequel. I'm assuming with the same alternate timeline. But if J.J. Abrams and company expect us to believe that it's Spock with the romantic tension with Uhura and not Kirk well, let's just say the message boards are going nuts.
jokes.setup: Ben trying to be boring by talking about Star Trek
jokes.punchline: His genuine passionate nerdiness about the topic
jokes.explanation: Good character moment where Ben can't help being passionate about nerdy topics even when trying to be boring.

dialogue.speaker: Ben
dialogue.line: You know, Star Trek is actually quite fascinating from a philosophical standpoint. The Prime Directive raises interesting questions about cultural intervention and—wait, sorry, I'm doing it again, aren't I? I'm being a nerd.

dialogue.speaker: Ben
dialogue.line: But seriously, the original series had incredible storytelling, and the way they used science fiction to explore real social issues was revolutionary. Did you know that the first interracial kiss on television was between Kirk and Uhura? That was groundbreaking!

dialogue.speaker: Ben
dialogue.line: And don't even get me started on the technical specifications of the Enterprise. The warp core, the dilithium crystals—it's all so elegant in its design. I could talk about this for hours.

jokes.text: I want to take you both home and...bend you over and just...at the same time.
jokes.setup: Joan's drunken sexual aggression
jokes.punchline: The extremely inappropriate threesome proposition
jokes.explanation: Peak cringe moment that works because of how uncomfortable it makes everyone.

jokes.text: It's not about the business anymore, man. She's a disaster. We've got to make sure she gets home safe.
jokes.setup: Tom abandoning his business goals
jokes.punchline: His genuine concern overriding self-interest
jokes.explanation: Good character development moment showing Tom's better nature emerging.

dialogue.speaker: Tom
dialogue.line: You know what? I'm shutting down Grawndy's. I know it's my dream, but... Leslie needs me right now. And that's more important.

jokes.text: Say what you will about Joan, but she knows how to decorate a bedroom. Wow. Oh, my God. Where the hell am I? I just want to see how soft these sheets are. One second. Tom. No. Come on. Let's go.
jokes.setup: Getting Joan to her bedroom
jokes.punchline: Tom being distracted by the luxury despite the situation
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Tom's materialism even in inappropriate situations.

dialogue.speaker: Tom
dialogue.line: Wow, this Egyptian cotton is incredible. Feel this.

dialogue.speaker: Ben
dialogue.line: Tom, focus. We need to get Joan to bed.

dialogue.speaker: Tom
dialogue.line: I am focused. I'm focused on the thread count. This is 1,200 thread count, Ben. Do you know what that means?

dialogue.speaker: Ben
dialogue.line: It means you need to stop touching the sheets and help me.

dialogue.speaker: Tom
dialogue.line: It means I've been living a lie, Ben. A lie of substandard bedding.

jokes.text: I also got this dude's briefcase. It was just laying there. Kind of a panic move. I believed in you. But you should put that back. Okay.
jokes.setup: Andy successfully getting the birth certificate
jokes.punchline: His casual admission to stealing a briefcase
jokes.explanation: Good Andy moment showing his chaotic problem-solving and casual criminality.

dialogue.speaker: Andy
dialogue.line: Yeah, I got it! I got the birth certificate!

dialogue.speaker: Leslie
dialogue.line: Andy, that's amazing! How did you manage that?

dialogue.speaker: Andy
dialogue.line: Oh, I just walked into the records office, found the file, and grabbed it.

dialogue.speaker: Leslie
dialogue.line: Wait, you just took it?

dialogue.speaker: Andy
dialogue.line: Yeah, well, I may have also taken a briefcase that wasn't mine on the way out.

dialogue.speaker: Leslie
dialogue.line: You stole a briefcase?

dialogue.speaker: Andy
dialogue.line: I don't know, it was just sitting there. Seemed important.

jokes.text: I'm from Eagleton.
jokes.setup: Leslie learning the truth about her birthplace
jokes.punchline: Her horrified realization
jokes.explanation: Good emotional payoff for the episode's central conflict. Leslie's horror is palpable.

dialogue.speaker: Leslie
dialogue.line: Wait, so I wasn't born in Eagleton?

dialogue.speaker: Leslie
dialogue.line: I was born in Pawnee? Oh my God, I'm a Pawnee native. I'm basically a traitor to everything I've ever believed in.

jokes.text: Pawnee Hospital was overrun with raccoons at the time, so we had to go to the next closest hospital.
jokes.setup: Explaining why Leslie was born in Eagleton
jokes.punchline: The ridiculous raccoon infestation excuse
jokes.explanation: Excellent absurdist detail that's perfectly Pawnee. The raccoon infestation is specific and believable for this universe.

dialogue.speaker: Leslie's Mother
dialogue.line: We were living in Eagleton at the time because of the raccoon infestation in Pawnee.

dialogue.speaker: Leslie
dialogue.line: Mom, there was no raccoon infestation.

dialogue.speaker: Leslie's Mother
dialogue.line: That's what we told you.

jokes.text: I'm the liar. I'm worse than a liar. I'm an... Eagletonian.
jokes.setup: Leslie's guilt about misleading people
jokes.punchline: The dramatic pause before 'Eagletonian' as the worst thing possible
jokes.explanation: Great escalation and timing. The pause makes 'Eagletonian' the ultimate insult in Leslie's mind.

dialogue.speaker: Leslie
dialogue.line: I feel terrible about misleading all those people. I mean, what I did was really dishonest and unethical, and I just... I can't believe I would do something so wrong. I mean, the only thing worse than what I did would be if I were from...

dialogue.speaker: Leslie
dialogue.line: Eagleton.

jokes.text: Well, this guy came in. He got his hand stuck in a Pringles can, and he tried to cut himself out. Was there a lot of blood? What kind of blade did he use? Three and a half inch. Serrated. Buckets of blood.
jokes.setup: Ann telling a medical story
jokes.punchline: April and Andy's morbid fascination and detailed questions
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing April and Andy's dark interests. The Pringles can detail is absurdly specific.

dialogue.speaker: Ann
dialogue.line: So I was examining this patient and found this huge cyst, and I had to drain it.

dialogue.speaker: April
dialogue.line: How huge? Like a golf ball?

dialogue.speaker: Andy
dialogue.line: Did it make a sound when you drained it? Like a whoosh?

dialogue.speaker: April
dialogue.line: What color was the fluid?

dialogue.speaker: Ann
dialogue.line: Why are you both so excited about this?

jokes.text: Everyone in the universe loves a gross medical story. Boom! I win!
jokes.setup: Andy's plan to get April and Ron to talk working
jokes.punchline: His triumphant declaration of victory
jokes.explanation: Good payoff to Andy's earlier goal. His pride in the simple achievement is endearing.

dialogue.speaker: Andy
dialogue.line: I did it! I got April and Ron to talk to each other. I am a genius. A beautiful, idiot genius.

jokes.text: When people get too chummy with me, I like to call them by the wrong name to let them know I don't really care about them. That's a genius move. Thank you. You're welcome, Lester.
jokes.setup: Ron explaining his social strategy
jokes.punchline: Immediately demonstrating it on Andy who complimented him
jokes.explanation: Excellent setup and immediate payoff. Perfect Ron logic and the timing is flawless. The 'Lester' delivery is perfect.

dialogue.speaker: Ron
dialogue.line: I have no need for small talk. I do not play the game. When someone compliments me, I simply nod once and move on.

dialogue.speaker: Andy
dialogue.line: That's a great strategy, Ron. You're really a man of few words.

dialogue.speaker: Ron
dialogue.line: *nods once*

jokes.text: Where you were born is a piece of trivia. Where you're from? That's what makes you who you are. And you are from Pawnee. You're more Pawnee than anyone I know.
jokes.setup: Discussing Leslie's dilemma about revealing the truth
jokes.punchline: Ben's heartfelt but also pragmatic advice
jokes.explanation: Good character moment that's both funny and touching. The distinction between birthplace and home is well-made.

dialogue.speaker: Ben
dialogue.line: Look, I know this is hard. Leslie cares deeply about doing the right thing, but sometimes the right thing and the smart thing aren't the same. You have to weigh the consequences. Will telling the truth actually help anyone, or will it just blow everything up? Because if it's the latter, then maybe you sit on it for now.

jokes.text: Damn it. He's right. Well said. Thanks, Ron. You're welcome, Steve.
jokes.setup: Ron agreeing with Ben's advice
jokes.punchline: Ron immediately using the wrong name strategy on Ben
jokes.explanation: Excellent immediate callback to Ron's strategy. The 'Steve' is perfectly Ron and the timing is great.

dialogue.speaker: Ron
dialogue.line: You know what, Ben? That's solid advice. I'm going to start calling you by the wrong name.

dialogue.speaker: Ron
dialogue.line: From now on, you're Gerald.

dialogue.speaker: Ben
dialogue.line: My name is Ben.

dialogue.speaker: Ron
dialogue.line: Not anymore, Gerald.

jokes.text: Well, thank you for getting me home last night. I wasn't feeling very well. Because of some bad seafood that I ate. Yes. That's probably what it was.
jokes.setup: Joan awkwardly addressing her drunken behavior
jokes.punchline: The transparent lie about seafood food poisoning
jokes.explanation: Good cringe moment showing Joan's inability to acknowledge her behavior. The seafood excuse is pathetically transparent.

dialogue.speaker: Joan
dialogue.line: I apologize for my behavior last night. I must have had some bad seafood.

dialogue.speaker: Tom
dialogue.line: Joan, we were at Applebee's. You ordered a salad.

jokes.text: When I was nine years old, I broke my arm sledding on that giant hill behind Kernston's Rubber Nipple factory. You know, Nipple Hill.
jokes.setup: Leslie proving her Pawnee credentials with childhood stories
jokes.punchline: The casual mention of 'Rubber Nipple factory' and 'Nipple Hill'
jokes.explanation: Excellent world-building detail that's both funny and perfectly Pawnee. The casual delivery makes it funnier.

dialogue.speaker: Leslie
dialogue.line: I was born at Pawnee General Hospital, which is right next to the old Rubber Nipple factory. My parents actually met at Nipple Hill, which is what we called the hill behind the factory. So yeah, I'm about as Pawnee as they come.

jokes.text: Do you remember when you peed your pants in second grade? Why did you bring that up? I said yes. You stuck your underwear in your drawer and you got real embarrassed, and then the teacher came over and pulled them out? And everybody called you 'the gerbil' because you always smelled like soiled wood chips?
jokes.setup: Leslie proving she knows Jim from childhood
jokes.punchline: The increasingly specific and embarrassing details
jokes.explanation: Good escalation showing Leslie going too far with personal details. The gerbil nickname is perfectly humiliating.

dialogue.speaker: Leslie
dialogue.line: Jim Boehmer! From Lincoln High! You used to wear those glasses with the tape on them, and you had that weird rash on your neck that you'd scratch until it bled.

dialogue.speaker: Jim
dialogue.line: How do you know that?

dialogue.speaker: Leslie
dialogue.line: And you were allergic to dairy, but you'd eat it anyway because you wanted to impress that girl Jennifer, and then you'd have to go to the nurse's office and get your stomach pumped.

dialogue.speaker: Jim
dialogue.line: Okay, that's enough.

dialogue.speaker: Leslie
dialogue.line: Plus you wore the same pair of khakis three days in a row, and there was that incident in tenth grade where you peed yourself in the cafeteria because you were too shy to ask for the bathroom pass.

jokes.text: But somewhere, in some town, there really are the best waffles in the world. So delicious and rich and golden brown that anyone who tasted them would decide never to leave that town. Somewhere, those waffles exist. Why can't it be here?
jokes.setup: Leslie's speech about why she loves Pawnee
jokes.punchline: The passionate waffle metaphor for civic pride
jokes.explanation: Excellent character moment that's both funny and touching. The waffle metaphor is perfectly Leslie and genuinely moving.

dialogue.speaker: Leslie
dialogue.line: I love Pawnee. I love it with all of my heart. It's the most wonderful place in the world. Look, some people love waffles, some people love pancakes. But I love Pawnee. It's like a waffle - golden, crispy on the outside, soft and fluffy on the inside, and it makes you happy.

jokes.text: Leslie? Here's the thing. I've been all over the state. Indianapolis, Bloomington, Lafayette, Muncie, Gary, all the places you mention in the book. And I've interviewed all the people you mentioned. And so far, there is not one incorrect fact.
jokes.setup: Jerry reporting back on his fact-checking mission
jokes.punchline: The revelation that he's been doing unnecessary work all along
jokes.explanation: Good Jerry moment showing his dedicated but unnecessary work. The irony that he's been wasted is typical.

dialogue.speaker: Jerry
dialogue.line: I've been fact-checking everything the department says for the last six months.

dialogue.speaker: Leslie
dialogue.line: That's great, Jerry. What have you found?

dialogue.speaker: Jerry
dialogue.line: Well, turns out we don't actually need to fact-check anything. We're the government. Nobody checks us.

jokes.text: He just seems so happy. I didn't say anything.
jokes.setup: Jerry enthusiastically continuing his pointless mission
jokes.punchline: Donna and Leslie's decision to let him continue being happy
jokes.explanation: Sweet character moment showing their protective attitude toward Jerry. The kindness makes it funny.

dialogue.speaker: Donna
dialogue.line: You know what? Let him be happy.

dialogue.speaker: Leslie
dialogue.line: Yeah, I agree. It's not hurting anybody.

tmdb_overview: Leslie's book about Pawnee comes out just in time for her campaign. Ann tries to bond with Ron and April.
insight: Small-town politics comedy grounds 72 jokes in character dynamics over spectacle.
